hero.subtitle: Precise ESP32-based filament scale — reads NFC tags automatically, reports weight and spool info to Home Assistant and Spoolman.

features.label: What FilaScale does
features.title: Everything for your filament management
features.subtitle: From precise weight measurement to automatic spool detection — FilaScale integrates seamlessly into your maker setup.

weight.title: Precise Weight Measurement
weight.desc: HX711-based load cell with automatic tare. Calibrate directly from Home Assistant — no reflashing required.

nfc.title: NFC Tag Detection
nfc.desc: Reads filament tags across multiple open standards: FilaMan, OpenSpool, OpenPrintTag and BambuLab RFID. Place spool — data appears instantly.

ha.title: Home Assistant
ha.desc: Native ESPHome integration with auto-discovery. Entities for weight, spool info and NFC status are immediately available in your dashboard.

display.title: Display Support
display.desc: Supports ST7920 128×64 (Ender3/5 display) and LCD1602 16×2. Read weight, remaining filament and material type right at the scale.

howto.label: How it works
howto.title: Up and running in three steps

steps.title: Build the hardware
steps.desc: ESP32 + HX711 load cell + NFC reader (PN532 or PN5180) + display of your choice. All components are inexpensive and widely available.

steps.title: Flash the firmware
steps.desc: Directly in your browser via the Web Installer — no USB driver, no terminal. Select your variant, click, done.

steps.title: Place spool & go
steps.desc: FilaScale connects to your Wi-Fi. Home Assistant discovers the device automatically. Put a spool on — all data appears instantly.

opensource.label: Open Source
opensource.title: By the community, for the community
opensource.desc: FilaScale is fully open source under the MIT license. ESPHome firmware, hardware schematics and documentation are freely available for everyone — to use, adapt and improve.

install.title: Web Installer
install.subtitle: Install FilaScale directly in your browser — no USB driver, no terminal.
reqList: Google Chrome or Microsoft Edge (Web Serial API required)
ESP32 development board connected via USB
USB cable with data lines (not charge-only)
install.step1: Connect ESP32 to your computer via USB
install.step2: Click "Install" and select the COM port
install.step3: Wait for the installation to complete
install.step4: After reboot: configure Wi-Fi via the "filascale" hotspot
install.browserWarning: ⚠️ Web Serial is not supported by this browser. Please use Chrome or Edge.
install.notSupported: Your browser does not support the Web Serial API.

docs.title: Documentation
docs.subtitle: Complete documentation for hardware, firmware and NFC standards

footer.desc: Open source filament scale for the 3D printing community. ESPHome · Home Assistant · MIT License.
